meal.title: Saucy Chicken and Cabbage Satay Bowls
meal.source: What to Cook When You Don't Feel Like Cooking

meal.title: BBQ Chickpea Bean Salad with Date BBQ Sauce

ingredients.item: coconut aminos (or reduced-sodium soy sauce/tamari), divided
canned black beans, drained and rinsed
red bell pepper, cored and diced

steps: Add the dates to a glass bowl and cover with boiling water. Soak for 15 minutes.

Preheat oven to 425°F. Toss chickpeas with 1 tbsp coconut aminos, 1 tbsp olive oil, 1 tsp garlic powder, 1 tsp onion powder, 1/2 tsp chili powder, 1/2 tsp smoked paprika, and 1/2 tsp salt on a large sheet pan. Bake until crisp, 20–22 minutes, tossing halfway.

Make the date BBQ sauce: blend soaked dates, 1/4 cup coconut aminos, tomato paste, apple cider vinegar, 2 tbsp olive oil, 1/2 tsp each of chili powder/garlic powder/onion powder/smoked paprika/salt, and 1/4 cup warm water until completely smooth. Add water 1 tbsp at a time to thin if needed.

Assemble the salad: combine black beans, corn, cabbage, bell pepper, and cherry tomatoes in a large bowl. Top with baked chickpeas and squeeze lime over. Pour BBQ sauce over everything and toss to coat.
meal.notes: Refrigerates well for up to 4 days. Serves 6 — expect leftovers for Thursday.
